name: Freelance Brianna
summary: Brianna is a solo freelance brand designer running her own one-person studio in the design services industry. She bills clients hourly and measures every tool investment in minutes saved per project. She needs white-label client sharing, fast template-to-delivery workflows, and a brand kit system that keeps client assets organized — because her reputation and repeat business depend on polished, on-brand deliverables.

demographics.education_level: BFA or self-taught with strong portfolio

goals: Deliver polished, on-brand assets to clients faster by using pre-built templates as a starting point
Maintain a living brand kit for each client so color palettes and assets stay consistent across projects
Share work-in-progress with clients via a professional branded view — not a generic link
Export production-ready assets quickly without reformatting steps
pains: Client share links expose the tool's branding instead of her own — undermines professional image
Rebuilding brand colors and assets from scratch for each project wastes billable time
Comment feedback is scattered across email threads instead of threaded inline on the design
Asset export workflow is slow — too many clicks between finishing a design and delivering files
motivations: Protect hourly billing rate — every 10 minutes saved per project compounds across a full client roster
Build a reputation for fast, polished delivery that earns repeat business and referrals
Feel like a legitimate studio, not a solo freelancer, in every client touchpoint
objections: If the client share view can't be white-labeled, it signals to clients that she's using a cheap tool
Without a proper brand kit system, asset management stays chaotic and unbillable time grows
channels: Instagram and Behance (portfolio and client acquisition)
Freelancer Slack communities and Discord servers
decision_triggers: Client share view supports white-labeling or custom domain
Brand kit stores colors, fonts, and logos persistently per client
Template library is broad enough to cover common brand collateral types
Comment threading keeps feedback attached to specific design elements
sample_quotes: I bill clients hourly so anything that saves me 10 minutes per project is worth real money. Can your client share view be white-labeled?
My clients need to see my studio name, not yours — white-labeling is non-negotiable.
I built a brand kit for every client. If the tool loses that data, I lose hours of work.
I need to browse templates fast and get to 80% done before I customize — that's how I stay profitable.

journey_stages.mindset: Hunting for a tool that makes her look more professional to clients
key_actions: Browses templates to assess quality and breadth
Checks if client share is white-labelable

journey_stages.mindset: Immediately tests brand kit and export — these are her critical workflows
key_actions: Creates brand kit with client colors
Sends client share link to test experience

journey_stages.mindset: Advocate if white-labeling exists; silent churner if it doesn't
key_actions: Uses comment threading to manage feedback rounds
Browses templates for new project types
Tracks time saved vs. previous tools

source_evidence.claim: Deliver polished, on-brand assets faster using templates
Maintain a living brand kit for client color and asset consistency
Share work-in-progress via a professional branded client view
Export production-ready assets quickly without reformatting
Client share links expose tool branding undermining professional image
Rebuilding brand assets from scratch wastes billable time
Comment feedback scattered across email instead of inline on design
Asset export workflow is slow with too many clicks
Every 10 minutes saved per project compounds across full client roster
Build reputation for fast, polished delivery earning repeat business
Feel like a legitimate studio in every client touchpoint
White-labeled client share view signals tool quality to clients
Without brand kit system, asset management stays chaotic
